kannada:
ಊರೆಲ್ಲ ನೆಂಟರು | ಕೇರಿಯೆಲ್ಲವು ಬಳಗೆ |
ಧಾರಿಣಿಯಲ್ಲ ಕುಲದೈವನಾಗಿನ್ನು |
ಯಾರನ್ನು ಬಿಡಲೊ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
Urella nenTaru | kEriyellavu baLage |
dhAriNiyalla kuladaivanAginnu |
yArannu biDalo Sarvajna ||
translation: The whole village is my kin, and the entire street is my family; when the whole earth is the manifestation of the divine, whom can I possibly exclude, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ನಂದಿಯನು ಏರಿದನ | ಚಂದಿರನ ಸೂಡಿದನ |
ಕಂದನ ಬೇಡಿ ನಲಿದಾನು ನೆನೆವುತ್ತ |
ಮುಂದೆ ಹೇಳುವೆನು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
nandiyanu Eridana | chandirana sUDidana |
kandana bEDi nalidAnu nenevutta |
munde hELuvenu Sarvajna ||
translation: Meditating upon the one who rides the bull Nandi and wears the moon as an adornment—the one who rejoiced when asked for a child—I shall proceed with my recitations, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಅಂದು ಜೇಡರ ದಾಸ | ಹಿಂದೆ ನಾ ವರರುಚಿಯು |
ಇಂದು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ನಾ ನಿಮ್ಮ ಕರುಣದ |
ಕಂದ ಕಾಣಯ್ಯ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
andu jEDara dAsa | hinde nA vararuchiyu |
indu Sarvajna nA nimma karuNada |
kanda kANayya Sarvajna ||
translation: Once I was Jedara Dasimayya, before that I was Vararuchi; today, by your grace, I am Sarvajna, your own child, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮುನ್ನ ಕೈಲಾಸದಲಿ ಪನ್ನಗಧರನಾಳು |
ಎನ್ನಯ ಪೆಸರು ಪುಷ್ಪದತ್ತನು ಎಂದು |
ಮನ್ನಿಪರು ದಯದಿ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
munna kailAsadali pannagadharanALu |
ennaya pesaru pushpadattanu endu |
manniparu dayadi Sarvajna ||
translation: Formerly in Kailasa, as an attendant to the one who wears the serpent (Shiva), my name was Pushpadatta; thus they recognize me with grace, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಅಂದಿನ ಪುಷ್ಪದತ್ತ ಬಂದ ವರರುಚಿಯಾಗಿ |
ಮುಂದವ ಸಾಲೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞನೆಂದೆನಿಸಿ |
ನಿಂದವನು ನಾನೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
andina pushpadatta banda vararuchiyAgi |
mundava sAle Sarvajnendenisi |
nindavanu nAne Sarvajna ||
translation: The Pushpadatta of those days came as Vararuchi, and subsequently, having become known to the world as Sarvajna, that same person am I, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮಕ್ಕಳಿಲ್ಲವೆಂದು ಅಕ್ಕಮಲ್ಲಮ್ಮನು |
ದುಕ್ಕವ ಬಸವರಸಗರುಪಲು ಕಾಸಿಯ |
ಮುಕ್ಕಣ್ಣನೊಲಿದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
makkaLillavendu akkamallammanu |
dukkava basavarasagarupalu kAsiya |
mukkaNNanolida Sarvajna ||
translation: When Akkamallamma expressed her grief of being childless to Basavarasa, the three-eyed Lord of Kashi was pleased and granted a boon, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮಾಸೂರ ಬಸವರಸ ಕೂಸನೀಶನ ಕೇಳೆ |
ಕಾಶಿಯಾ ಅಭವನೊಳು ಪಡೆದ ವರವದು |
ಸೂಸಿತೆಂತೆನಲು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
mAsUra basavarasa kUsanIshana kELe |
kAshiyA abhavanoLu paDeda varavadu |
sUsitentenalu Sarvajna ||
translation: When Basavarasa of Masuru prayed to the Lord for a child, the boon obtained from the Lord of Kashi manifested; I shall tell how it flowed forth, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗಂಡಾಗಬೇಕೆಂದು ಪಿಂಡವನು ನುಂಗಲದು |
ರಂಡೆಮಾಳವ್ವೆಯೊಳು ಬರುವೆನೆಂದೆನಲು |
ಕಂಡದನು ಪೇಳ್ದೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
ganDAgabEkendu pinDavanu nungaladu |
ranDemALavveyoLu baruvenendenalu |
kanDadanu pELde Sarvajna ||
translation: Desiring a son, he swallowed the consecrated offering; as for how I came to be born through the widow Malavve, I have spoken of what was witnessed, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗಡಿಗೆ ಮಡಿಕೆಯಕೊಂಡು ಅಡಿಗೆಯಾವೇಳೆಯೊಳು |
ಬಿಡದೆ ಮಳೆಮೋಡ ಪೊಡೆಯಲು ಈರ್ವರಿಗೆ |
ತಡೆಬಿಡಲಾಯ್ತು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
gaDige maDikeyakonDu aDigeyAvELeyoLu |
biDade maLemODa poDeyalu Irvarige |
taDebiDAlAytu Sarvajna ||
translation: While busy with pots and pans at the time of cooking, as the rain clouds burst incessantly, the two were caught in a moment of sudden confusion, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಅಂಬಲೂರೋಳೆಸೆವ ಕುಂಬಾರಸಾಲೆಯಲಿ |
ಇಂಬಿನಾಕಳೆಮಾಳಿಯೊಳು ಬಸವರಸ- |
ನಿಂಬಿಟ್ಟನೆನ್ನ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
ambalUrOLeseva kumbArasAleyali |
imbinakaLemALiyoLu basavarasa- |
nimbiTTanenna Sarvajna ||
translation: In the potter's street of Ambalur, within the graceful Mali, Basavarasa placed his seed; thus was I conceived, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಬಿಂದುವನು ಬಿಟ್ಟುಹೋದಂದು ಬಸಿರಾದಳವ- |
ಳೊಂದಾಗಿ ಹೊತ್ತ ದಶಮಾಸವುದರದಿ |
ನಿಂದಾನು ಬೆಳದೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
binduvanu biTTuhOdandu basirAdaLava- |
Londaagi hotta dashamAsavudaradi |
nindAnu beLade Sarvajna ||
translation: From the day the seed was left, she became pregnant; for ten months she carried me in her womb, and there I grew, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹೆತ್ತವಳು ಮಾಳಿಯೆ | ನ್ನೂತ್ತಿ ತೆಗೆದವಳು ಬೆ- |
ನ್ನೊತ್ತಿ ಹಿಡಿದವಳು ಕಾಳಿಯು, ಮೊರದೊಳಗೆ |
ಬಿತ್ತರಿಸಿದವಳು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
hettavaLu mALiye | nnUtti tegedavaLu be- |
nnotti hiDidavaLu kALiyu, moradoLage |
bittarisidavaLu Sarvajna ||
translation: Mali was the mother who gave birth; Kali was the one who lifted me up and supported my back, and laid me out in the winnowing basket, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಬೆಳೆಯೆನ್ನದರಿದೆಂದು ಕಳೆಯಕಂಡವರೆಲ್ಲ |
ಮೊಳೆಯನಲ್ಲಿ ಸಸಿಯನರಿತು ತಾಂ ಮಾಳಿಯ |
ತಿಳಿವವರು ಅರಿಗು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
beLeyennadaridendu kaLeyakanDavarella |
moLeyanalli sasiyanaritu tAn mALiya |
tiLivavaru arigu Sarvajna ||
translation: While everyone sees the crop, none saw the spark of its beginning; only those who understand how a seedling sprouts can truly know the mother Mali, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗಂಡು ತನ್ನದು ಎಂದು | ಲಂಡೆ ಸುತನೆಂಬುವಳು |
ಖಂಡಪರಶುವಿನ ವರಪುತ್ರ ನಾನರೆ |
ಕಂಡುದನೆ ನುಡಿದೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
ganDu tannadu endu | lanDe sutanembuvaLu |
khanDaparashuvina varaputra nAnare |
kanDudane nuDide Sarvajna ||
translation: The woman claims this boy as her own son, but I am the boon-born son of Lord Shiva; I speak only the truth of what I have realized, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮಾಳನೂ ಮಾಳಿಯೂ ಕೂಳಿಂದ ಹೆಮ್ಮೆಯಲಿ |
ಕೇಳಲೆನ್ನಾರಮಗನೆಂದು, ನಾ ಶಿವನ |
ಮೇಳದವನೆಂದೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
mALanU mALiyU kULinda hemmeyali |
kELalennAramaganendu, nA shivana |
mELadavanende Sarvajna ||
translation: Mala and Mali may claim me as their son with great pride, but listen—I belong to the assembly of Shiva, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ತಾ, ಎಂದೆನಲ್ಲದೆ ತಾಯಿ ನಾನೆಂದೆನೆ? |
ತಾಯಿಯೆಂದಾನು ನುಡಿದೇನು ಪರಸ್ತ್ರೀಯ |
ತಾಯಿಯೆಂದೆಂಬೆ! ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
tA, endenallade tAyi nAnendene? |
tAyiyendAnu nudidEnu parastrIya |
tAyiyendembe! Sarvajna ||
translation: I acknowledged her, but did I call her 'my mother'? I address all other women as 'mother'; in that same sense, I spoke of her, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ನೂಕಿದರು ಅವರಾಗ ಕುಹಕತನದೊಳು ಬೇಗ |
ಲೋಕದೊಳಗೆಲ್ಲ ಕಂಡುದನು ನುಡಿದು ತಾ |
ನೇಕವಾಗಿಹನು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
nUkidaru avarAga kuhakatanadoLu bEga |
lOkadoLagella kanDudanu nuDidu tA |
nEkavAgihanu Sarvajna ||
translation: Though they pushed him away in their malice, he spoke of all he witnessed in the world and became one with the Ultimate, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮೀರಿಬೆಳೆಯಲಿ ತನುವು ಆರುಬಣ್ಣವನುಡಿಸಿ |
ಮೂರುರುಚಿದೋರಿ ಮರಸಿ ತಾ ತನ್ನನು |
ತೋರದೇ ಹೋದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
mIribeLeyali tanuvu ArubaNNavanuDisi |
mUruruchidOri marasi tA tannanu |
tOradE hOda Sarvajna ||
translation: As the body grows, draped in six colors and distracted by triple tastes, the soul forgets its true self and remains hidden, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಕುಲಗೆಟ್ಟಿವರು ಚಿಂತೆ ಯೊಳಗಿರ್ಪರಂತಲ್ಲ |
ಕುಲಗೆಟ್ಟು ಶಿವನ ಮರೆಹೊಕ್ಳ ಋಷಿಗಳು |
ಕುಲವಾದರಯ್ಯ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
kulageTTivaru chinte yoLagirpavantalla |
kulageTTu shivana marehokla rishigaLu |
kulavAdarayya Sarvajna ||
translation: Do not worry about those who have lost their caste; the sages who abandoned worldly status to seek refuge in Shiva became the highest lineage themselves, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹೊಲಸುಮಾಂಸದಹುತ್ತ ಎಲುವಿನಾ ಹಂದರವು |
ಹೊಲೆಬಲಿದ ತನುವಿನೊಳಗಿರ್ದುಮದರೊಳು |
ಕುಲವನರಸುವರೆ? ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
holasumAmsadahutta eluvinA handaravu |
holebalida tanuvinoLagirdumadaroLu |
kulavanarasuvare? Sarvajna ||
translation: In this mound of foul flesh and frame of bones, within a body bound by impurities—does one still go searching for caste? O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಎಲುವಿನೀಕಾಯಕ್ಕೆ ಸಲೆಚರ್ಮದಾಹೊದಿಕೆ |
ಮಲಮೂತ್ರ ಕ್ರಿಮಿಗಳೊಳಗಿರ್ದ ದೇಹಕೆ |
ಕುಲವಾವುದಯ್ಯ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
eluvinIkAyakke salecharmadAhodike |
malamUtra krimigaLoLagirda dEhake |
kulavAvudayya Sarvajna ||
translation: For this body made of bone and covered in a wrap of skin—a vessel for waste and germs—how can there be any talk of caste? O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮಾಸಿನೊಳು ಮುಸುಕಿರ್ದು ಮೂಸಿಬಹುದಾಸನವ |
ಹೇಸಿಕೆ ಮಲವು ಸೂಸುವದಕಂಡು ಕುಲ- |
ದಾಸೆಯನುಬಿಡರು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
mAsinoLu musukirdu mUsibohudAsanava |
hEsike malavu sUsuvadakanDu kula- |
dAseyanubiDaru Sarvajna ||
translation: Though covered in filth and seeing the stench of waste that flows from this seat of a body, people still refuse to give up their attachment to caste, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞನೆಂಬವನು ಗರ್ವದಿಂದಾದವನೆ? |
ಸರ್ವರೊಳೊಂದೊಂದು ನುಡಿಗಲಿತು ವಿದ್ಯದ |
ಪರ್ವತವೆ ಆದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
Sarvajnane mbavanu garvadindAdavane |
sarvaroLondondu nuDigalitu vidyada |
parvatave Ada Sarvajna ||
translation: Did the one called 'All-Knowing' become so out of pride? By learning one word from everyone he met, Sarvajna became a mountain of knowledge, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹೇಳಲರಿಯೆನು ನಾನು ಹೇಳೆನಲು ಹೇಳಿದೆನು |
ಬಾಳಲೋಚನನ ಶರಣರ ಆಳಾಗಿ |
ಹೇಳಿದೆನು ನೋಡ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
hELalariyenu nAnu hELenalu hELidenu |
bALalOchanana sharanara ALAgi |
hELidenu nODa Sarvajna ||
translation: I do not claim to know how to speak; I spoke only because I was asked. As a servant to the devotees of the three-eyed Lord, I have shared these words, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಎನ್ನ ಮನಸಿಗೆ ನೆನಹ ಪನ್ನಗಧರ ಕೊಟ್ಟ |
ಬಿನ್ನಣದಿಂದ ಸ್ತುತಿಗೈದು ಪೊಗಳುವೆ |
ಚನ್ನ ಮೂರುತಿಯ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
enna manasige nenaha pannagadhara koTTa |
binnaNadinda stutigaiudu pogaLuve |
channa mUrutiya Sarvajna ||
translation: The Lord who wears the serpent gave the spark to my mind; with that skill, I praise and sing of the beautiful form of the Divine, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗುರುವಿನಾವಿಸ್ತರದ ಪರಿಯನಾನೇನೆಂಬೆ! |
ಮೆರೆವ ಬ್ರಹ್ಮಾಂಡದೊಳ ಹೊರಗವ ಬೆಳೆಗಿ |
ಪರಿಪೂರ್ಣನಿಪ್ಪ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
guruvinAvistarada pariyAnAnEnEmbe |
mereva brahmAnDadoLa horagava beLegi |
paripUrNanippa Sarvajna ||
translation: How can I describe the vastness of the Guru? Shining both inside and outside the magnificent universe, the Guru exists in total perfection, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹರತನ್ನೊಳಿರ್ದುಗುರು ತೋರದೇ ತಿಳಿವುದೇ? |
ಮರದೊಳಗಗ್ನಿಯಿರುತಿರ್ದು ತನ್ನ ತಾ |
ನರಿಯದೇಕೆಂದ? ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
hara tannOLirdu guru tOradE tiLivudE |
maradoLagagniyirutirdu tanna tA |
nariyadEkenda Sarvajna ||
translation: Though Shiva resides within, can one know Him without the Guru's guidance? Just as fire exists within wood but does not know itself until sparked, so is the soul, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮೂರು ಕಣ್ಣೀಶ್ವರನ ತೋರಿಕೊಡಬಲ್ಲ, ಗುರು |
ಬೇರದರೊಳೊಂದು ತೆರನಿಲ್ಲ; ಗುರುಕರುಣ |
ತೋರುವುದು ಹರನ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
mUru kaNNEshwarana tOrikoDaballa guru |
bEradarOLondu teranilla gurukaruNa |
tOruvudu harana Sarvajna ||
translation: The Guru can show you the three-eyed Lord; there is no difference between them. It is through the Guru's grace that Shiva is revealed, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಜ್ಯೋತಿಯಿಂದಲಿನೇತ್ರ ರಾತ್ರಿಯೊಳು ಕಾಂಬಂತೆ |
ಸೂತ್ರದಿಂದಾತನರಿವಂತೆ ಶಿವನಗುರು- |
ನಾಥನಿಂದರಿಗು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
jyOtiyindalinEtra rAtriyoLu kAmbante |
sUtradindAtanarivante shivanaguru |
nAthanindarigu Sarvajna ||
translation: Just as a light allows the eye to see in the night, or as a thread leads to the source, one realizes Shiva only through the Lord-like Guru, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ವಿದ್ಯಕಲಿತಡೆ ಇಲ್ಲ ಬುದ್ಧಿಕಲಿತಡೆ ಇಲ್ಲ |
ಉದ್ಯೋಗ ಮಾಡಿದಡೆ ಇಲ್ಲ ಗುರುಮುಖ- |
ವಿದ್ದಲ್ಲದಿಲ್ಲ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
vidyakalitaDe illa buddhikalitaDe illa |
udyOga mADidaDe illa gurumukha |
viddalladilla Sarvajna ||
translation: Learning sciences is not enough, nor is mere intellect or hard work. Truth is not attained without looking toward the face of the Guru, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮಂಡೆಬೋಳಾದೊಡಂ ದಂಡಕೋಲ್ವಿಡಿದೊಡಂ |
ಹೆಂಡತಿಯಬಿಟ್ಟು ನಡೆದೊಡಂ ಗುರುಮುಖ |
ಕಂಡಲ್ಲದಿಲ್ಲ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
manDEbOLAdoDam danDakOlviDiDoDam |
henDatiyabiTTu nadedoDam gurumukha |
kanDalladilla Sarvajna ||
translation: Shaving one's head, carrying a staff, or leaving one's wife behind—none of these lead to the goal unless one has seen the face of the Guru, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಊರಿಂಗೆ ದಾರಿಯನು ಯಾರು ತೋರಿದಡೇನು |
ಸಾರಾಯದಾನಿಜವ ತೋರುವ ಗುರುವು |
ಯಾರಾದಡೇನು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
Uringe dAriyanu yAru tOridaDEnu |
sArAyadAnijava tOruva guruvu |
yArAdaDEnu Sarvajna ||
translation: What does it matter who shows the way to the village? The Guru who reveals the essence of truth—whoever they may be—is the one who matters, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಪರತತ್ವ ತನ್ನೊಳಗೆ ಎರವಿಲ್ಲದಿರುತಿರ್ದು |
ಪರದೇಶಿಯಂತೆ ಇರುತಿಪ್ಸಯೋಗಿಯನು |
ಪರಮಗುರುವೆಂಬೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
paratattva tannoLage eravilladirutirdu |
paradEshiyante irutipsayOgiyanu |
paramaguruvembe Sarvajna ||
translation: The yogi who holds the supreme truth within themselves without any duality, yet lives like a stranger to the world—I call such a one the supreme Guru, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹರನಿಚ್ಛೆಯಿಲ್ಲದಲೆ ಹರಿದು ಹೋಗದು ಪಾಪ |
ಹರಭಕ್ತಿಯುಳ್ಳ ಗುರುವರನೋರ್ವನೇ |
ನರ ದೈವವೆಂಬೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
haranichcheyilladale haridu hOgadu pApa |
harabhaktiyuLLa guruvaranOrvanE |
nara daivavembe Sarvajna ||
translation: Without the will of Hara, sins do not wash away; the noble Guru who possesses deep devotion to Hara is indeed a God in human form, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಬಂಧುಗಳು ಆದವರು ಬಂದುಂಡು ಹೋಗುವರು |
ಬಂಧನವಕಳೆಯಲರಿಯರು ಗುರುವಿಂದ |
ಬಂಧುಗಳುಂಟೆ? ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
bandhugaLu Adavaru bandunDu hOguvaru |
bandhanavakaLeyalariyaru guruvinda |
bandhugaLunTe? Sarvajna ||
translation: Relatives come and share a meal, then depart; they do not know how to break the bonds of worldly attachment. Is there any relative greater than the Guru? O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಜೀಯ! ಸದ್ಗುರುನಾಥ ! ಕಾಯಪುಸಿಯನೆ ತೋರಿ |
ಮಾಯಪಾಶವ ಹರಿಸುತ್ತ ಶಿಷ್ಯಂಗೆ |
ತಾಯಿಯಂತಾದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
jIya! sadgurunAtha! kAyapusiyane tOri |
mAyapAshava harisutta shishyange |
tAyiyantAda Sarvajna ||
translation: O Lord! The noble Guru, by showing the transience of the body and cutting through the nets of illusion, becomes like a mother to the disciple, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ತಂದೆಗೂ ಗುರುವಿಗೂ ಒಂದು ಅಂತರವುಂಟು |
ತಂದೆ ತೋರುವ ಸದ್ಗುರುವ; ಗುರುರಾಯ |
ಬಂಧನವ ಕಳೆವ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
tandegU guruvigU ondu antaravunTu |
tande tOruva sadguruva; gururAya |
bandhanava kaLeva Sarvajna ||
translation: There is a difference between a father and a Guru; the father introduces one to the noble Guru, but it is the Guru who liberates one from all bonds, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗುರುವಿಗೂ ದೈವಕ್ಕೂ ಹಿರಿದು ಆಂತರವುಂಟು |
ಗುರುತೋರ್ವ ದೈವದೆಡೆಗಳ ದೈವತಾ |
ಗುರುವ ತೋರುವದೆ? ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
guruvigU daivakkU hiridu antaravunTu |
gurutOrva daivadeDegaLa daivatA |
guruva tOruvade? Sarvajna ||
translation: There is a great difference between the Guru and God; the Guru points the way to God, but does God ever show the way to a Guru? O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹುಸಿವವನ ಬೇಹಾರ, ಕಸಹತ್ತಿದಾರಂಬ, |
ವಿಷಯವುಳ್ಳವನ ಗುರುತನಿವು ಮೂರು |
ಮಸಿವಣ್ಣವೆಂದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
husivavana bEhAra, kasahattidAramba |
vishayavuLLavana gurutanivu mUru |
masivaNNavenda Sarvajna ||
translation: The business of a liar, agriculture overgrown with weeds, and the spiritual initiation (upadesha) of a sensual person—these three are as worthless as charcoal, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ದರುಶನಗಳಾರರಿಂ ಪುರುಷರು ಮೂವರಿಂ |
ಪರತತ್ವದಿರವು ಬೇರೆಂದು ತೋರಿದ |
ಗುರುತಾನೆ ಹರನು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
darushanagaLArarim purusharu mUvarim |
paratattvadiravu bErendu tOrida |
gurutAne haranu Sarvajna ||
translation: Among the six systems of philosophy and the three types of souls, the Guru who reveals that the Supreme Truth is beyond all these is none other than Shiva himself, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ನಂಬುವುದು! ನಿನಗೇಕೆ? ಹಂಬಲವು ಪರಶಿವನ? |
ನಂಬಿ ಗುರುದೈವವನಗಸ್ತ್ಯ ಮುನಿತಾ |
ನಂಬುಧಿಯ ಸವಿದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
nambuvudu! ninagEke? hambalavu parashivana? |
nambi gurudaivavanagastya munitA |
nambudhiya savida Sarvajna ||
translation: Have faith! Why do you merely long for Shiva? By placing absolute faith in his Guru, the sage Agastya was able to drink the entire ocean, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಪರಮನಯ್ಮೊಗವುಳಿದು ನೆರವಿಯನೊಲ್ಲದೇ |
ನರರೂಪಧರಿಸಿ ಗುರುವಾಗಿ ಎರೆವಗೆ |
ಸರಿಯಹರಕಾಣೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
paramanaymogavuLidu neraviyanolladE |
nararUpadharisi guruvAgi erevage |
sariyaharakANe Sarvajna ||
translation: Shedding his five faces and divine form, taking the shape of a human Guru to bless his devotees—there is no one equal to Hara who does this, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಪರಮನಾ ರೂಪದಲಿ ನೆರವೀಯಲರಿಯದೇ |
ನರನಾಗಿ ಬಂದು ವರವೀವ ಗುರುವಿಗೆ |
ಸರಿಯಾರಕಾಣೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
paramanA rUpadali neravIyaliyariyadE |
naranAgi bandu varavIva guruvige |
sariyArakANe Sarvajna ||
translation: Knowing that he cannot grant salvation in his absolute form, the Supreme Lord comes as a human Guru to bestow boons; there is no equal to such a Guru, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಸುರತರುವು ಮರನಲ್ಲ ಸುರಭಿಯೊಂದಾವಲ್ಲ |
ಪರುಷ ಪಾಷಾಣದೊಳಗಲ್ಲ ಗುರುರಾಯ |
ನರರೊಳಗಲ್ಲ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
surataruvu maranalla surabhiyondAvalla |
parusha pAshANadoLagalla gururAya |
nararoLagalla Sarvajna ||
translation: The celestial tree (Kalpataru) is no ordinary tree, the divine cow (Surabhi) is no ordinary cow, the philosopher's stone is no ordinary rock, and the noble Guru is no ordinary human, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗುರುವ ನರನೆಂದವಗೆ ಹರನ ಶಿಲೆಯೆಂದವಗೆ |
ಕರಣಪ್ರಸಾದ ಎಂಜಲೆಂದವನಿಗೆ |
ನರಕ ತಪ್ಪುವದೆ? ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
guruva naranendavage harana shileyendavage |
karaNaprasAda enjalendavanige |
naraka tappuvade? Sarvajna ||
translation: For the one who considers the Guru a mere human, the Lord (Shiva) a mere stone, and the consecrated offering (Prasada) mere leftovers—can such a person ever escape hell? O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹಿರಿಯನಾನೆನಬೇಡ ಗುರುವನಿಂದಿಸಬೇಡ |
ಬರೆವರ ಕೂಡ ಹಗೆಬೇಡ, ಬಂಗಾರ |
ದೆರವು ಬೇಡೆಂದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
hiriyanAnenabEDa guruvanindisabEDa |
barevara kUDa hagebEDa, bangAra |
deravu bEDenda Sarvajna ||
translation: Do not boast that you are great, do not insult the Guru, do not bear enmity towards writers/scholars, and do not beg for gold, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಒಂದೂರೊಳಿರಲು ಗುರು ವಂದನೆಯ ಮಾಡದೇ |
ಸಂದಿಸಿ ಕೂಳನುಣತಿಪ್ಪವನ ಬದುಕು |
ಹಂದಿಯಂತೆಂದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
ondUroLiralu guru vandaneya mADadE |
sandisi kULanuNatipavana baduku |
handiyantenda Sarvajna ||
translation: Living in the same village as the Guru and yet failing to offer salutations, one who simply busies himself with eating—his life is like that of a pig, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಉರಿ, ಯುದಕ ಮಾರುತವು, ಉರಗ, ಹರಿ, ನಾಗವೂ |
ಗುರುವಾಜ್ಞೆಗಂಜಿನಿಲುವವು; ಇದನು ನರ- |
ರರಿಯದೇ ಕೆಡಗು! ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
uri, yudaka mArutavu, uraga, hari, nAgavU |
guruvAjneganjiniluvavu; idanu nara- |
rariyadE keDagu! Sarvajna ||
translation: Fire, water, wind, serpents, lions, and elephants—all stand in awe of the Guru's command; failing to realize this, humans bring about their own ruin, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಕೊರಡು ಸುರತರುವಾಯ್ತು ಬರಡು ನೆರೆಹಯಿನಾಯ್ತು! |
ಮರುಳು ತಂಡಗಳು ಪ್ರಣವನುಚ್ಛರಿಸುವ |
ಗುರುವಾಜ್ಞೆ ಗಂಜಿ! ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
koraDu surataruvAytu baraDu nerehayinAytu! |
maruLu tanDagaLu praNavanuchcharisuva |
guruvAjne ganji! Sarvajna ||
translation: A dry log turned into a celestial tree, and barren land became a lush crop! Even the ignorant began to chant the 'Pranava' (Om), all out of reverence for the Guru's command, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗುರುಸೇವೆ ಕೈಕೊಂಡು ಹಿರಿದಾಗಿ ಮಾಡದಲೆ |
ಹರಿದೇರಿ ಮುಕ್ತಿ ಪಡೆಯುವಡೆ ಅದು ತಾನು! |
ಹಿರಿದಾದ ತೆವಕೆ? ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
gurusEve kaikonDu hiridAgi mADadale |
haridEri mukti paDeyuvaDe adu tAnu! |
hiridAda tevake? Sarvajna ||
translation: Taking up the service of the Guru but failing to perform it with greatness, yet expecting to attain salvation—is that not a grand delusion? O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಎತ್ತಾಗಿ ತೊತ್ತಾಗಿ ಹಿತ್ತಲದ ಗಿಡನಾಗಿ |
ಮತ್ತೆ ಪಾದದ ಕೆರವಾಗಿ ಗುರುವಿನ |
ಹತ್ತಿಲಿರು ಎಂದ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
ettAgi tottAgi hittalada giDanAgi |
matte pAdada keravAgi guruvina |
hattiliru enda Sarvajna ||
translation: Be like an ox, like a servant, like a plant in the backyard, or like the sandals on his feet—remain close to the Guru with such humility, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗುರುವಚನವುಪದೇಶ ಗುರುವಚನ ಪರಭಕ್ತಿ |
ಗುರುವಚನ ಮೋಕ್ಷಪದವದು ಗುರುವಚನ |
ಪರಮಾರ್ಥವಯ್ಯ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
guruvachanavupadEsha guruvachana parabhakti |
guruvachana mOkshapadavadu guruvachana |
paramArthavayya Sarvajna ||
translation: The Guru's word is the true teaching, it is supreme devotion; the Guru's word is the path to liberation, it is the ultimate truth, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಬಳ್ಳಿಗುರುಡರು ಕೂಡಿ ಹಳ್ಳವನು ಬಿದ್ದಂತೆ |
ಒಳ್ಳೆಯ ಗುರುವಿನುಪದೇಶವಿಲ್ಲದಿರೆ |
ಎಲ್ಲಿಯದು ಮುಕ್ತಿ? ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
baLLigurudaru kUDi haLLavanu biddante |
oLLeya guruvinupadEshavilladire |
elliyadu mukti? Sarvajna ||
translation: Like a chain of blind men following each other into a pit, without the guidance of a good Guru, where is the possibility of salvation? O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ನರಕ ಪಾಪಿಷ್ಯರಿಗೆ ಸುರಲೋಕನಿಷ್ಕರಿಗೆ |
ಗುರುಬೋಧೆಯಿಂದ ಜಗಮುಳಿದುದಲ್ಲದೆ |
ಉರಿದು ಹೋಗಿಹುದು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
naraka pApishyharige suralOkanishkarige |
gurubO dheyinda jagamuLidudallade |
uridu hOgihudu Sarvajna ||
translation: Hell is for the sinners and the celestial world is for the pure; if not for the Guru's teaching, this world would have been consumed by fire instead of being saved, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಬೆಳ್ಳುಳ್ಳಿ ಪೊಡೆದಿಹರೆ ಹೋಳಪ್ಪತೆರನಂತೆ |
ಒಳ್ಳೆಯ ಗುರುವಿನುಪದೇಶದಿಂಪಾಪ |
ಹೊಳ್ಳಿಬೀಳುವದು! ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
beLLuLLi poDedihare hOLappateranante |
oLLeya guruvinupadEshadimpApa |
hoLLibIluvadu! Sarvajna ||
translation: Just as a clove of garlic splits apart when pressed, through the noble Guru's initiation, sins shatter and fall away, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮೊಸರು ಕಡೆಯಲು ಬೆಣ್ಣೆ ಯೊಸೆದು ತೋರುವ ತೆರದಿ |
ಹಸನುಳ್ಳ ಗುರುವಿನುಪದೇಶದಿಂ ಮುಕ್ತಿ |
ವಶವಾಗದಿಹುದೆ? ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
mosaru kaDeyalu beNNe yosedu tOruva teradi |
hasanuLLa guruvinupadEshadim mukti |
vashavAgadihude? Sarvajna ||
translation: Just as butter emerges when curd is churned, will not liberation come under one's control through the initiation of a virtuous Guru? O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ತುಪ್ಪವಾದಾ ಬಳಿಕ ಹೆಪ್ಪನೆರೆದವರುಂಟೆ? |
ನಿಷ್ಪನ್ನನಾದ ಗುರೂಪದೇಶದಿಂ |
ತಪ್ಪದದು ಮುಕ್ತಿ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
tuppavAdA baLika heppaneredavarunTe? |
nishpannanAda gurUpadEshadim |
tappadadu mukti Sarvajna ||
translation: Once milk has turned into ghee, does anyone add curd to it again? Similarly, through the initiation of a perfected Guru, liberation is certain, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮರಹುಳ್ಳಮನುಜರಿಗೆ ತೆರನಾವುದರಿವುದಕೆ? |
ಕರಿಗೊಂಡ ಬೆಳಲಹಣ್ಣಂತೆ, ಗುರುಬೋಧೆ |
ನೆರೆಗೊಳ್ಳಬೇಕು! ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
marahuLLamanujarige teranAvudarivudake? |
karigonDa beLalahaNnante, gurubOdhe |
neregoLLabEku! Sarvajna ||
translation: For humans filled with forgetfulness, how can they ever know the truth? Like the wood-apple swallowed by an elephant (which stays whole but is empty inside), the Guru's teaching must fully permeate the soul, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಸಾಣೆಕಲ್ಲೊಳು ಗಂಧ ಮಾಣದೇ ಎಸೆವಂತೆ |
ಜಾಣ ಸದ್ಗುರುವಿನುಪದೇಶದಿಂ ಮುಕ್ತಿ |
ಕಾಣಿಸುತ್ತಿಹುದು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
sANekalloLu gandha mAnadE esevante |
jANa sadguruvinupadEshadim mukti |
kANisuttihudu Sarvajna ||
translation: Just as fragrance emanates steadily when sandalwood is rubbed on a grindstone, liberation becomes visible through the initiation of a wise and noble Guru, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಬೊಮ್ಮವೆಂಬುದು ತಾನು ಒಮ್ಮಾರುದೂರವೇ? |
ಒಮ್ಮೆ ಸದ್ಗುರುವಿನುಪದೇಶವಪ್ಪಡೆ |
ಗಮ್ಮನೇ ಮುಕ್ತಿ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
bommave mbudu tAnu ommArudUravE? |
omme sadguruvinupadEshavappaDe |
gammanE mukti Sarvajna ||
translation: Is Brahman (the Supreme Reality) so far away? If one receives the initiation of a noble Guru even once, liberation is attained instantly and gracefully, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹೇಳಿದ್ದೇ ಹೇಳುವರು, ಕೇಳಿದ್ದೇ ಕೇಳುವರು, |
ಕೇಳಿದ್ದೆ ಕೇಳಿ ಸುಖಿಸುವರು; ಗುರುಗಳ |
ಹೇಳಿಕೆಯೆ ಬೇರೆ! ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
hELiddE hELuvaru, kELiddE kELuvaru |
kELidde kELi sukhisuvaru; gurugaLa |
hELikeye bEre! Sarvajna ||
translation: People keep saying what has been said and hearing what has been heard, finding joy in the repetitive; but the Guru's unique teaching is entirely different! O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಎಂಟು ಕಂಟಕರಿಂಗೆ ನೆಂಟರುಗಳಯ್ವರಿಗೆ |
ಗಂಟಲಗಾಳನಾಗಿಪ್ಪಗುರು ನೀಲ- |
ಕಂಠನಾಗಿಕ್ಕು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
enTu kanTakarinnge nenTarugaLayvarige |
ganTalagALanAgippa guru nIla- |
kanThanAgikku Sarvajna ||
translation: For the eight inner enemies and the five senses (relatives), the Guru acts like a hook in the throat to restrain them; such a Guru is indeed the blue-throated Lord Shiva himself, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಮೂರರಿಂದಲಿ ಹೊರಗೆ ತೋರಿರುವದನು ಗುರುವು |
ಆರರಿಂದೊಳಗೆ ತೋರಿದಡೆ ಶಿಷ್ಯತಾ |
ನಾರಡಿಗೊಂಬ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
mUrarindali horage tOriruvadanu guruvu |
ArarindoLage tOridaDe shishyatA- |
nAraDigomba Sarvajna ||
translation: When the Guru reveals that which is seen through the three (Gunas) outside, within the six (inner Chakras), the disciple attains complete spiritual fulfillment, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಒಂದರೊಳಗೆಲ್ಲವೂ ಸಂದಿಸಿರುವದನು ಗುರು |
ಚಂದದಿಂತೋರಿಕೊಡದಿರೆ ಶಿಷ್ಯನವ |
ಕೊಂದನೆಂದರಿಗು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
ondaroLegellavU sandisiruvadanu guru |
chandadintOrikoDadire shishyanava |
kondanendarigu Sarvajna ||
translation: If the Guru does not beautifully demonstrate how everything is united in the One (Supreme Reality), know that the disciple remains spiritually lost, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಹಂದಿಯಾ ಚಂದನದ ಗಂಧವನು ಅರಿವುದೇ |
ಒಂದನು ತಿಳಿಯಲರಿಯದಾ ಗುರುವಿಂಗೆ |
ನಿಂದೆಯೇ ಬಹುದು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
handiyA chandanada gandhavanu arivudE |
ondanu tiliyaliyariyadA guruvinnge |
nindeyE bahudu Sarvajna ||
translation: Does a pig ever appreciate the fragrance of sandalwood? Similarly, a Guru who does not comprehend the One Truth will only invite public criticism, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ಗುರುವೆ ನಿಮ್ಮನು ನೆನೆದು ಉರಿವಕಿಚ್ಚನು ಹೊಗಲು |
ಉರಿತಗ್ಗಿ ಉದಕ ಕಂಡಂತೆ ನಿಮ್ಮಯ |
ಕರುಣವುಳ್ಳರಿಗೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
guruve nimmanu nenedu urivakichchanu hogalu |
uritaggi udaka kanDante nimmaya |
karuNavuLLarige Sarvajna ||
translation: O Guru, for those who have your grace, even if they enter a blazing fire while meditating on you, the heat subsides like cool water, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ದುರ್ಗಿಮಾರಿಯು ಚಂಡಿಯಗ್ಗದಾ ಶಕ್ತಿಗಳು |
ಸದ್ಗುಣ ಸತ್ಯ ಮುಸುಕಿಹರ ಮುಟ್ಟಿ ವಿದು |
ಸದ್ಗುರುನಿನಾಜ್ಞೆ ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
durgimAriyu chanDiyaggadA shaktigaLu |
sadguNa satya musukihara muTTividu |
sadguruninAjne Sarvajna ||
translation: Even powerful forces like Durgi, Mari, and Chandi cannot touch those who are covered by truth and virtue; such is the command of the noble Guru, O Sarvajna.

kannada:
ವಿಷಯಕ್ಕೆ ಕುದಿಯದಿರು ಅಶನಕ್ಕೆ ಹರಿಯದಿರು |
ಅಸಮಾಕ್ಷನಡಿಯ ಬಿಡದಿರು ಗುರುಕರುಣ |
ವಶವರ್ತಿಯಹುದು ಸರ್ವಜ್ಞ ||
transliteration:
vishayakke kudiyadiru ashanakke hariyadiru |
asamAkshanaDiya biDadiru gurukaruNa |
vashavartiyahudu Sarvajna ||
translation: Do not burn with sensual desires, do not run frantically after food; do not leave the feet of the three-eyed Lord, and the Guru's grace will surely be yours, O Sarvajna.
